topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the old park bench felt strangely heavy beneath my fingers. I’d been sketching the overgrown ivy when I noticed it—a thick, dark wallet lying half-hidden beneath a cluster of fallen leaves. My heart gave a sudden, uneven thump against my ribs. I picked it up, the weight of it surprising me. When I finally managed to open the clasp, the sight inside made my breath catch. Stacks of banded currency spilled out, a cascade of crisp bills that seemed impossibly large. I stared down at the sheer volume of money, my mind refusing to process the reality of it. I stood up slowly, my hands trembling slightly as I looked around the quiet, empty path, wondering who had left such a treasure behind.

[story 2]

"What in the world?" I muttered under my breath, running a hand through my already messy hair. The wallet had been tucked into the pocket of my coat, and now, lying on the pavement near the entrance of the coffee shop, it was undeniable. I knelt down, my movements jerky, and pulled it out. The sheer thickness of the cash inside made my vision swim for a moment. I flipped through the contents—ID, cards, and then the money. My fingers brushed against the paper, and a cold knot tightened in my stomach. I looked up at the passersby, expecting some kind of reaction, but everyone seemed focused on their own destinations. I just sat back on my heels, completely unable to figure out what to do next.

[story 3]

It was tucked inside a discarded newspaper, a forgotten offering on the curb. I found it while taking out the recycling. When I finally pulled the wallet free, the sheer quantity of cash hit me like a physical blow. I held it up, the paper money shimmering under the afternoon sun. My mouth went dry. I tried to rationalize it—a prank, a mistake—but the reality of the stacks refused to settle into any logical explanation. I kept turning the wallet over in my hands, my gaze darting from the money to the empty street, a strange, hollow feeling spreading through my chest. I felt rooted to the spot, as if the world had suddenly tilted on its axis.
